title: Calculate the Volume of Snow
text: First, calculate the volume of the snow that moved during the avalanche. Use the formula for volume: Volume $$=$$ Area * Thickness.

title: Calculate the Volume of Snow
text: The area of the snow is $$100$$ $$m$$ by $$500$$ $$m$$, and the thickness is $$0.4$$ $$m$$. Calculate the volume.

title: Calculate the Mass of Snow
text: Now, calculate the mass of the snow using the density and the volume you just calculated. Use the formula: Mass $$=$$ Density * Volume.

title: Calculate the Mass of Snow
text: The density of the snow is $$350$$ $$\frac{kg}{m^3}$$, and the volume is $$20000$$ $$m^3$$. Calculate the mass.

title: Calculate the Velocity of the Avalanche
text: Next, calculate the velocity of the avalanche. Use the formula: Velocity $$=$$ Distance / Time.

title: Calculate the Velocity of the Avalanche
text: The distance is $$1000$$ $$m$$, and the time is $$5.5$$ s. Calculate the velocity.

title: Calculate the Average Momentum
text: Finally, calculate the average momentum of the avalanche using the formula: Momentum $$=$$ Mass * Velocity.

title: Calculate the Average Momentum
text: The mass is $$7000000$$ kg, and the velocity is $$181.8181818$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$. Calculate the momentum.
